airQuality.providerNote: Air quality is in the Moderate range. Sensitive patients may notice mild symptoms.

pollen.providerNote: Pollen activity is moderate. Routine allergy guidance for sensitive families.

respiratoryIllness.wastewaterTrend: Regional wastewater signal available at metro level only; not yet broken out by ZIP for the North Dallas area.
respiratoryIllness.providerNote: Regional respiratory activity is stable across RSV, flu, and COVID.

entries.name: RSV
entries.parentNote: RSV is in its off-season. Very few cases circulating right now.
entries.providerNote: CDC NREVSS (HHS Region 6 weekly % positivity) — week ending 2026-06-20: 0.9% positivity, stable vs. prior weeks.

entries.name: Influenza A
entries.parentNote: Flu A activity has tapered off for the season.
entries.providerNote: CDC national clinical-lab surveillance — week ending 2026-06-20: 0.7% positivity for influenza overall (Type A/B subtypes not separated in this feed), decreasing vs. prior weeks.

entries.name: Influenza B
entries.parentNote: Flu B is uncommon right now.
entries.providerNote: CDC national clinical-lab surveillance — week ending 2026-06-20: 0.7% positivity for influenza overall (Type A/B subtypes not separated in this feed), decreasing vs. prior weeks.

entries.name: COVID-19
entries.parentNote: COVID cases are low. Wastewater signal is also stable.
entries.providerNote: CDC NREVSS (HHS Region 6 weekly % positivity) — week ending 2026-06-20: 0.9% positivity, rising vs. prior weeks.

entries.name: Rhinovirus / Enterovirus
entries.parentNote: The common cold (rhinovirus) is the most active virus in our area right now.
entries.providerNote: CDC NREVSS (HHS Region 6 weekly % positivity) — week ending 2026-06-20: 22.4% positivity, stable vs. prior weeks.

entries.name: Human Metapneumovirus (hMPV)
entries.parentNote: hMPV is winding down after its spring peak.
entries.providerNote: CDC NREVSS (HHS Region 6 weekly % positivity) — week ending 2026-06-20: 0.4% positivity, decreasing vs. prior weeks.

entries.name: Parainfluenza
entries.parentNote: Parainfluenza is rising — this is the main cause of croup (barky cough).
entries.providerNote: CDC NREVSS (HHS Region 6 weekly % positivity) — week ending 2026-06-20: 1.9% positivity, stable vs. prior weeks.

entries.name: Adenovirus
entries.parentNote: Adenovirus activity is low right now.
entries.providerNote: CDC NREVSS (HHS Region 6 weekly % positivity) — week ending 2026-06-20: 2.8% positivity, rising vs. prior weeks.

entries.name: Norovirus
entries.providerNote: No public live regional surveillance feed for Norovirus. CDC NoroSTAT and Texas DSHS bulletins update episodically; check those manually for outbreak alerts.

entries.name: Rotavirus
entries.parentNote: Rotavirus is rare thanks to widespread vaccination.
entries.providerNote: No public live regional surveillance feed for Rotavirus. CDC NoroSTAT and Texas DSHS bulletins update episodically; check those manually for outbreak alerts.

entries.name: Hand, Foot & Mouth Disease
entries.parentNote: Hand, foot & mouth disease is picking up as we head into summer — daycare and camp settings especially.
entries.providerNote: No public live regional surveillance feed for Hand, Foot & Mouth Disease. CDC NoroSTAT and Texas DSHS bulletins update episodically; check those manually for outbreak alerts.

entries.name: Fifth Disease (Parvovirus B19)
entries.parentNote: Fifth disease is around right now — look for the 'slapped-cheek' rash. Most kids do fine.
entries.providerNote: No public live regional surveillance feed for Fifth Disease (Parvovirus B19). CDC NoroSTAT and Texas DSHS bulletins update episodically; check those manually for outbreak alerts.

communityVirusWatch.providerNote: Latest CDC NREVSS / FluView data for week ending 2026-06-20.

items.pediatricRelevance: First-line for AOM, strep pharyngitis, CAP in many pediatric patients.
items.suggestedProviderAction: Confirm pharmacy availability before prescribing high-volume liquid formulations; consider alternative concentrations.

items.pediatricRelevance: Common second-line for AOM and sinusitis when amoxicillin is insufficient.
items.suggestedProviderAction: Verify availability with local pharmacy before sending; have alternate regimens ready.

items.pediatricRelevance: Common penicillin alternative for AOM and sinusitis.
items.suggestedProviderAction: Monitor for changes if amoxicillin demand surges.

items.pediatricRelevance: Core rescue therapy for pediatric asthma and reactive airway disease.
items.suggestedProviderAction: Reinforce spacer use and asthma action plans; remind families to refill before respiratory season peaks.

items.pediatricRelevance: Used for in-clinic nebulizer treatments and home neb therapy in younger children.
items.suggestedProviderAction: Track on-hand clinic stock; prefer MDI + spacer when clinically appropriate.

items.pediatricRelevance: Influenza treatment in eligible pediatric patients during flu season.
items.suggestedProviderAction: Revisit if regional flu activity rises.

items.pediatricRelevance: Common ADHD therapy; shortages affect refill workflows and family planning.
items.suggestedProviderAction: Discuss formulation alternatives with family; coordinate with pharmacy before changing dose or product.

items.pediatricRelevance: Alternate ADHD therapy; shortages affect refill and switching options.
items.suggestedProviderAction: Plan refill timing carefully; consider documented alternative regimens per family.

items.vaccineRelevance: MMR-preventable. Highly contagious; airborne. Confirm MMR1 (12-15 mo) and MMR2 (4-6 yr) at every visit.
items.suggestedProviderAction: Verify MMR status, prompt catch-up doses, and review measles isolation/notification protocol with staff.
items.thresholdRationale: CDC defines a measles outbreak as 3 or more epidemiologically-linked cases (CDC Manual for the Surveillance of Vaccine-Preventable Diseases, Ch. 7).

items.vaccineRelevance: DTaP/Tdap-preventable. Infants under 2 mo are highest-risk. Confirm caregiver Tdap (cocooning).
items.suggestedProviderAction: Low threshold for testing prolonged paroxysmal cough; confirm DTaP series and Tdap for adolescents.
items.thresholdRationale: 183 cases YTD vs 1893 same period last year (at or below historical baseline).

items.vaccineRelevance: HepA-preventable. Two-dose series starting at 12 mo.
items.suggestedProviderAction: Confirm HepA series at well visits; emphasize for travel to endemic regions.
items.thresholdRationale: 29 cases YTD vs 47 same period last year (at or below historical baseline).

items.suggestedProviderAction: Confirm two-dose varicella status at school-age visits; counsel on rash isolation.
items.thresholdRationale: 223 cases YTD vs 215 same period last year (above prior-year baseline per CDC observed-vs-expected method).

items.vaccineRelevance: MMR-preventable. Outbreaks often occur in close-contact settings (camps, schools).
items.suggestedProviderAction: Confirm MMR2 in school-age and adolescents; review parotitis differential during outbreaks.
items.thresholdRationale: 3 case(s) year-to-date — below the threshold (5) at which year-over-year comparison is statistically meaningful.

items.suggestedProviderAction: Confirm PCV series; review post-splenectomy and high-risk indications.
items.thresholdRationale: 22 cases YTD vs 39 same period last year (at or below historical baseline).

operationalRecommendations: Hold a few same-day sick visit slots if call volume rises this week.
Keep allergy and asthma guidance materials ready for families calling about cough or wheeze.
Reinforce asthma action plan use during visits for wheezing or asthma-history patients.
Prepare a simple fever/cough triage script for front desk and MAs.
Check local pharmacy availability before sending prescriptions for constrained medications.
Review respiratory season parent messaging (handwashing, sick-day plans, when to call).
sources: Google Pollen API (tree, grass, weed pollen levels)
CDC NREVSS / NoroSTAT / Texas DSHS (community virus watch)
CDC NNDSS / Texas DSHS (vaccine-preventable disease surveillance)
